dynamics.description: Volume control, dynamic contrast, crescendo/diminuendo shaping

anchors.1: Severely lacking in dynamics. E.g.: The teacher compares the student's dramatic interpretation to Boris Godunov's sinister character who says 'Begone, Satan!', calling it totally unnatural and criminal to the music.
anchors.3: Adequate dynamics; competent but not distinctive.

timing.description: Tempo, rubato, ritardando, fermata, rhythmic patterns, syncopation, and pulse

anchors.1: Severely lacking in timing. E.g.: Teacher warns that playing too fast makes notes inaudible to the listener and references mature Arrau and Horowitz who slowed down for clarity without losing virtuosity. Advises a speed that allows everything to be heard, stimulating the audience more effectively.
anchors.3: Adequate timing; competent but not distinctive.

pedaling.description: Sustain and una corda pedal usage, clarity, and textural effects

anchors.1: Severely lacking in pedaling. E.g.: All these little ornaments are not nice because you put the pedal on them, making the sound dirty. It has to sound clean.
anchors.3: Adequate pedaling; competent but not distinctive.

articulation.description: Note attack/release, staccato, legato, accents, and touch variety

anchors.1: Severely lacking in articulation. E.g.: Teacher instructs that each note must start with a consonant attack like 'pam, pam, pam', with proper key contact akin to a string player's bow on string, not surface hitting, emphasizing piano playing should never be percussive due to abrupt attacks.
anchors.3: Adequate articulation; competent but not distinctive.

phrasing.description: Melodic line shaping, phrase arcs, breathing, and structural direction

anchors.1: Severely lacking in phrasing. E.g.: Teacher instructs to produce a different sound for the 'Pom' like deep, dark trombones with sordino, deaf and funeral-like, emphasizing tense silence with fermata on the silence.
anchors.3: Adequate phrasing; competent but not distinctive.

interpretation.description: Tone quality, voicing, timbre, expression, character, ornamentation, and interpretive choices

anchors.1: Severely lacking in interpretation. E.g.: The playing is too weak and too fingery; play more legato and in one motion rather than separate finger articulations, as this motive returns later.
anchors.3: Adequate interpretation; competent but not distinctive.
